spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates how fixed touch targets fail accessibility guidelines compared to dynamically scaled ones.

interactive.rationale: Provides an excellent real-time auditing table that updates pass/fail results dynamically based on slider scale.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Directly supports the blurb's description of a dynamic component library audit.

edge_cases.rationale: Scaling down below 1.0x (e.g. 0.85x) causes smaller scaled buttons to drop under the WCAG 24px minimum floor.

references.rationale: Missing the mandatory chromestatus.com/feature/5328467685801984 reference link.

design_system.rationale: Appropriately utilizes CSS variables and design tokens from styles.css.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus reference link to Touch Target Checker concept page
openQuestions.detail: The concept page is missing the mandatory chromestatus.com/feature/5328467685801984 link, which is a repository requirement for all feature and concept pages.

openQuestions.title: Enforce WCAG 24px floor using CSS max() for scaled buttons
openQuestions.detail: When simulating scale factors below 1.0x (e.g. 0.85x), base components like the Chip (24px) shrink to ~20px, violating WCAG 2.5.8 guidelines. Using CSS max(24px, calc(... * var(--sim-scale))) would resolve this.

summary: A highly interactive utility checking touch targets against WCAG 2.5.8 under simulated font scaling. It exposed a missing mandatory ChromeStatus reference link and a minor edge-case flaw where scaling down below 1.0x violates the 24px accessibility minimum.
